mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in payroll employment exceeding 150,000 signals a strengthening labor market that boosts consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with payroll employment exceeding 150,000 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production exceeding 2% signals a weakening supply chain that compresses profit margins and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with industrial production decline exceeding 2% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing starts exceeding 1.2 million signals a strengthening housing market that boosts consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with housing starts exceeding 1.2 million are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the federal funds rate and a 2-month decline in the CPI signal a coordinated easing monetary and disinflationary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with coordinated easing monetary and disinflationary cycle are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in payroll employment exceeding 150,000 and a 2-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5% signal a strengthening labor market and a potential peak in the business cycle that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with strengthening labor market and potential peak in the business cycle are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production exceeding 2% and a 2-month decline in the GDP signal a weakening supply chain and a potential recession that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with weakening supply chain and potential recession are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real disposable personal income exceeding 2% and a 2-month increase in the savings rate exceeding 0.5% signal a strengthening consumer spending and a potential peak in the business cycle that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with strengthening consumer spending and potential peak in the business cycle are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
